MX.header: Namecheap → Advanced DNS → Mail Settings: set 'Custom MX', then add:

SRV.header: Namecheap → Advanced DNS → Add New Record → SRV Record:

TXT.header: Namecheap → Advanced DNS → Add New Record → TXT Record:

CNAME.header: Namecheap → Advanced DNS → Add New Record → CNAME Record:

MX.header: Squarespace → Domains → your domain → DNS → Custom Records → add:

SRV.header: Squarespace → Domains → your domain → DNS → Custom Records → add:

TXT.header: Squarespace → Domains → your domain → DNS → Custom Records → add:

CNAME.header: Squarespace → Domains → your domain → DNS → Custom Records → add:

MX.header: Cosmotown → Domains → your domain → DNS records → 'MX(Mail Exchange Record)' section → + Quick Add. Leave the Host field blank for the root domain — Cosmotown auto-fills your domain (do NOT type @; the blank Host below is intentional); enter Points to with no trailing period:

SRV.header: Cosmotown's customer panel has no SRV section — contact Cosmotown support (the 'Support' link, top-right) and ask them to add this SRV record for you:

TXT.header: Cosmotown → Domains → your domain → DNS records → 'TXT(TXT Record)' section → + Quick Add. Leave the Host field blank for the root domain (do NOT type @; the blank Host below is intentional); enter the value WITHOUT surrounding quotes (the panel adds them) and preserve its exact upper/lowercase:

CNAME.header: Cosmotown → Domains → your domain → DNS records → 'CNAME(CNAME Record)' section → + Quick Add (the panel stores the target with a trailing dot automatically):

MX.header: bunny.net → DNS → select your DNS Zone → Add Record. Leave Hostname empty for the root domain (bunny lists it as your domain; do NOT type @). Bunny has one Value field with no separate Priority field — enter the priority and mail host together:

SRV.header: bunny.net → DNS → select your DNS Zone → Add Record. Bunny has one Value field with no separate Priority/Weight/Port fields — enter the whole SRV value (priority weight port target):

TXT.header: bunny.net → DNS → select your DNS Zone → Add Record. Leave Hostname empty for the root domain (do NOT type @):

CNAME.header: bunny.net → DNS → select your DNS Zone → Add Record:

MX.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. Spaceship → your domain → Advanced DNS → DNS records → + Add record → MX. Host is @ for the root domain (Spaceship auto-appends your domain):

SRV.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. Spaceship → your domain → Advanced DNS → DNS records → + Add record → SRV:

TXT.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. Spaceship → your domain → Advanced DNS → DNS records → + Add record → TXT. Host is @ for the root domain:

CNAME.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. Spaceship → your domain → Advanced DNS → DNS records → + Add record → CNAME:

MX.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. GoDaddy → Domain Portfolio → your domain → DNS → DNS Records → Add New Record → Type MX. Name is @ for the root domain (GoDaddy uses @, not a blank field):

SRV.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. GoDaddy → Domain Portfolio → your domain → DNS → DNS Records → Add New Record → Type SRV. GoDaddy splits the record into separate Service and Protocol fields; Name is @ for the root domain:

TXT.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. GoDaddy → Domain Portfolio → your domain → DNS → DNS Records → Add New Record → Type TXT. Name is @ for the root domain:

CNAME.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. GoDaddy → Domain Portfolio → your domain → DNS → DNS Records → Add New Record → Type CNAME:

MX.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. IONOS → Domains & SSL → the gear next to your domain under Actions → DNS → ADD RECORD → MX. Host name is @ for the root domain:

SRV.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. IONOS → Domains & SSL → the gear next to your domain under Actions → DNS → ADD RECORD → SRV. IONOS splits the record into separate Service and Protocol fields (Protocol is a TCP/UDP/TLS dropdown — choose TCP); Host name is @ for the root domain and Points to is the target:

TXT.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. IONOS → Domains & SSL → the gear next to your domain under Actions → DNS → ADD RECORD → TXT. Host name is @ for the root domain. For SPF: IONOS may already have added its own SPF TXT (v=spf1 include:_spf-us.ionos.com ~all) — replace/merge it so only one SPF record remains:

CNAME.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. IONOS reaches CNAME via Domains & SSL → the gear next to your domain → Manage Subdomains → the gear on the subdomain → DNS → ADD RECORD → CNAME. Enter the target without a trailing dot:

MX.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. OVHcloud → Web Cloud → DNS zones → select your domain → DNS zone tab → Add an entry → MX. Leave Sub-domain empty for the root domain (OVH appends your domain); enter the Target with a trailing dot:

SRV.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel (the SRV wizard layout is not yet screenshotted). OVHcloud → Web Cloud → DNS zones → select your domain → DNS zone tab → Add an entry → SRV. Keep the whole _service._protocol label in Sub-domain; enter the Target with a trailing dot:

TXT.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. OVHcloud → Web Cloud → DNS zones → select your domain → DNS zone tab → Add an entry → TXT. Leave Sub-domain empty for the root domain. For SPF, choose TXT (not the 'OVHcloud SPF' button, which injects include:mx.ovh.com):

CNAME.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. OVHcloud → Web Cloud → DNS zones → select your domain → DNS zone tab → Add an entry → CNAME. Enter the Target WITH a trailing dot (OVH appends your domain otherwise):

MX.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. Hover → your domain → DNS section → Add a record → Type MX. Hostname is @ for the root domain (Hover uses @, per its docs):

SRV.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel (the SRV layout is from a live Edit-DNS-Record screenshot). Hover → your domain → DNS section → Add a record → Type SRV. Hover splits the record into separate Service and Protocol fields; leave the Subdomain (optional) field BLANK for the root domain (do NOT type @ — the panel shows your domain beside the empty box):

TXT.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. Hover → your domain → DNS section → Add a record → Type TXT. Hostname is @ for the root domain:

CNAME.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. Hover → your domain → DNS section → Add a record → Type CNAME (Hover can't set a CNAME on the root domain, but every DKIM record is on a subdomain, so that's fine). Enter the target without a trailing dot:

MX.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. DigitalOcean → Networking → Domains → select your domain → Create a record → Record Type MX. Hostname is @ for the root domain ('Use @ to create the record at the domain root'); enter the mail server with a trailing dot (DigitalOcean appends your domain otherwise):

SRV.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel (the SRV layout is from a live Create-a-record screenshot). DigitalOcean → Networking → Domains → select your domain → Create a record → Record Type SRV. Keep the whole _service._protocol label in Hostname; enter 'Will direct to' (the target) WITH a trailing dot or DigitalOcean appends your domain:

TXT.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. DigitalOcean → Networking → Domains → select your domain → Create a record → Record Type TXT (SPF is a plain TXT record here). Hostname is @ for the root domain:

CNAME.header: UNVERIFIED — confirm the field names against your panel. DigitalOcean → Networking → Domains → select your domain → Create a record → Record Type CNAME. Enter 'Is an alias of' WITH a trailing dot (DigitalOcean appends your domain otherwise):
